heading: Bureau of Prisons tribal prisoner program
label: 1302a Bureau of Prisons tribal prisoner program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , the Director of the Bureau of Prisons shall establish a program under which the Bureau of Prisons shall accept offenders convicted in tribal court pursuant to (as amended by this section), subject to the conditions described in paragraph (2).March 15, 2022section 1302 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: As a condition of participation in the program described in paragraph (1), the tribal court shall submit to the Attorney General a request for confinement of the offender, for approval by the Attorney General (or a designee) by not later than 30 days after the date of submission.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: B Limitations
children.text: Requests for confinement shall be limited to offenders convicted of a violent crime (comparable to the violent crimes described in ) for which the sentence includes a term of imprisonment of 1 or more years.section 1153(a) of title 18

children.heading: Custody conditions
children.label: C Custody conditions
children.text: The imprisonment by the Bureau of Prisons shall be subject to the conditions described in , regarding the custody of State offenders, except that the offender shall be placed in the nearest available and appropriate Federal facility, and imprisoned at the expense of the United States.section 5003 of title 18

children.heading: Cap
children.label: D Cap
children.text: The Bureau of Prisons shall confine not more than 100 tribal offenders at any time.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The applicable tribal government shall retain the authority to rescind the request for confinement of a tribal offender by the Bureau of Prisons under this paragraph at any time during the sentence of the offender.

children.heading: Return to tribal custody
children.label: B Return to tribal custody
children.text: On rescission of a request under subparagraph (A), a tribal offender shall be returned to tribal custody.

children.heading: Reassessment
children.label: 4 Reassessment
children.text: If tribal court demand for participation in this program exceeds 100 tribal offenders, a representative of the Bureau of Prisons shall notify Congress.
